checks.a_analysis: I have compared the output text with the source PDF page by page. All headings ('NC Parcels', 'October 7, 2025', 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', 'Thank you!') and body text (legend items, sources, contact info) are directly derived from the document. There are no invented sections, hallucinated body text, or made-up conclusions.

The email addresses listed in the 'Contacts' section on page 3 ('katie.doherty@rutherfordcountync.gov', 'relkins@sosnc.gov', 'elizabeth.daniel@nc.gov') match the source PDF exactly. There is no other contact information provided in the source to check.

The source PDF contains 3 pages. The output JSON contains 3 sections that correspond to the three slides. All content from the title slide, the status map/legend/sources slide, and the closing/contact slide are included.

There are no formal tables in the source document. The legend on page 2, which functions as a data list, has been correctly converted into a list format in the JSON with all rows ('Processing for 2025 Q4 (15)', '2025 Q4 (6)', '2025 Q3 (66)', '2025 Q2 (10)', '2024 Q4 (2)', '2024 Q2 (1)') represented.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I verified the text content of each section. Section 2 (Page 2) contains all the textual elements found in the source: the main title, the date, the full legend, and the sources paragraph. While individual county abbreviations on the map image itself are not listed as text elements, the map is preserved as an image, maintaining the information. Section 3 (Page 3) preserves the closing message and all contact emails.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: All dates, counts, and names were cross-checked. The date 'October 7, 2025' is consistent throughout. The numerical counts in the legend items are accurate. No wording changes that alter information or meaning were detected.

checks.a_analysis: I examined the source PDF page by page against the provided JSON output. Page 1 contains the title and date, which appear correctly once in Section 1. Page 2 contains a title, date, map, legend, and sources; these are all accounted for in Section 2. The date 'October 7, 2025' appears twice in the source (Pages 1 and 2) and is correctly represented twice in the output without extraneous duplication. No text blocks or sections are repeated incorrectly.

The heading hierarchy is preserved and logically structured. Section 1 uses an H1 for the document title. Section 2 uses an H1 for the page title and an H2 for the sub-header date, maintaining a proper nested structure. Section 3 uses an H1 for the closing title. Levels are consistent and no levels are skipped.
The source document does not contain any tables. The data on Page 2 is a map legend, which has been appropriately converted into a list structure rather than a table.

The reading order follows the logical progression of the presentation slides. On Page 2, the pipeline has prioritized the text-based legend and sources before the map image and secondary logos. This is an acceptable adjustment for accessibility, ensuring that the contextual descriptions (legend) are provided before the visual element (map).

The map legend on Page 2 has been converted into an unordered list. The list items accurately reflect the entries in the legend ('Processing for 2025 Q4 (15)', '2025 Q4 (6)', etc.), preserving the meaning and grouping of the source data.

The contact information on Page 3 (email addresses) is present as plain text within a paragraph. While they are not hyperlinked in the JSON, the JSON schema for text content used here does not appear to support inline link types (only styles like bold/italic). All contact strings match the source exactly, and no fake links have been added.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the slide-based structure of the source PDF into a single-column scrolling layout. The visual flow of information—from title to the status map and then the closing section—is maintained. The transformation of the map legend from an embedded graphic element to a structured list is an accessibility improvement that preserves the visual organization and clarity of the document.
No data tables are present in either the source PDF or the converted HTML output.

A visual defect exists regarding the logos at the bottom of the document. While the first instance of the NCDIT logo and the status map image are rendered correctly, the NCDIT logos from the footer area of PDF pages 2 and 3 are corrupted. In the HTML output, these appear as two large, solid black rectangular blocks instead of the intended graphics. This is a major visual failure of brand-identifying imagery.
checks.b_evidence_source: NCDIT logos located in the bottom-right corner of pages 2 and 3 of the source PDF.
checks.b_evidence_output: Two large, solid black rectangular blocks appearing at the end of the HTML document.
checks.b_evidence_location: Bottom of the document, following the 'Thank you!' section.

checks.a_analysis: There is no duplication of actual body content. Section headings appear in the Table of Contents and at the start of their respective sections in the body, which is expected behavior for accessible document navigation.
